message.content:
Edit Tools/jjk/JJD-GallopsData.adoc to add 'n' as the action code for standard notch commits.

Changes needed:
1. Line ~1950: Change "optional for standard notch commits" to "required for all commits"
2. Line ~1954: Change `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX: commit message` to `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: commit message`
3. Line ~1959: Update or remove "No action code for standard commits" text
4. Line ~1970-1973: Add `* \`n\` — Notch: standard commit` to the pace-level action codes list (after the existing A, W, F, d codes)
5. Line ~2034: Change "absent for standard notch commits" to "present for all commits"
6. Line ~2070: Change `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX: message` to `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:n: message`

Read the file first to find exact line numbers, then make all edits.

   104→:jjdo_rein:           <<jjdo_rein,jjx_rein>>
   105→:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
   106→:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>
   107→:jjdo_slate:          <<jjdo_slate,jjx_slate>>
   108→:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>

   110→
   111→// Section headers
   112→:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>
   113→:jjds_behavior:         <<jjds_behavior,Behavior:>>

   115→:jjds_exit_uniform:     <<jjds_exit_uniform,Exit Status:>>

   117→
   118→// Serialization
   119→:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
   120→:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>
   121→
   122→// end::mapping-section[]
   123→
   124→== Overview
   125→
   126→The
   127→{jjdgr_gallops}
   128→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   129→It tracks
   130→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   131→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   132→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   133→(discrete actions).
   134→Each
   135→{jjdpr_pace}
   136→maintains a history of
   137→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   138→(plan refinements).
   139→
   140→== Design Principles
   141→
   142→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   143→
   144→All access to the
   145→{jjdgr_gallops}
   146→goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   147→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   148→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   149→
   150→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   151→The internet is full of bad bash.
   152→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   153→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   154→
   155→=== Git as Journal
   156→
   157→The
   158→{jjdgr_gallops}
   159→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   160→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   161→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   162→
   163→The
   164→{jjdkr_tack}
   165→history within each
   166→{jjdpr_pace}
   167→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   168→Git has the full history, but
   169→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   170→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   171→
   172→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   173→
   174→New
   175→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   176→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   177→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   178→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   179→
   180→The
   181→{jjdhm_order}
   182→array contains full
   183→{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   184→Each
   185→{jjdt_coronet}
   186→embeds its parent
   187→{jjdt_firemark}
   188→(redundant within a single
   189→{jjdhr_heat}).
   190→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   191→
   192→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   193→
   194→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   195→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   196→{jjdgr_gallops}
   197→file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   198→{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   199→If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   200→
   201→The pattern:
   202→[source,bash]
   203→----
   204→lock_acquire
   205→vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   206→lock_release
   207→----
   208→
   209→This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   210→
   211→=== Deterministic Serialization
   212→
   213→Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   214→For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   215→This ensures that reordering paces via
   216→{jjdo_rail}
   217→produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   218→
   219→=== Identity Encoding
   220→
   221→{jjdt_firemark}
   222→(`₣` prefix) and
   223→{jjdt_coronet}
   224→(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   225→The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   226→Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   227→
   228→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   229→
   230→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   231→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   232→
   233→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   234→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   235→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   236→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   237→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   238→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   239→* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   240→
   241→== Types
   242→
   243→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   244→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   245→{jjdt_firemark}::
   246→The identity of a
   247→{jjdhr_heat}.
   248→Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
   249→The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   250→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   251→Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.
   252→
   253→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   254→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   255→{jjdt_coronet}::
   256→The identity of a
   257→{jjdpr_pace}.
   258→Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
   259→Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   260→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   261→encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   262→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   263→Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
   264→+
   265→*Reassignment:* When a
   266→{jjdpr_pace}
   267→is moved between
   268→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   269→via
   270→{jjdo_draft},
   271→the old
   272→{jjdt_coronet}
   273→becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   274→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   275→{jjdhm_seed}.
   276→The new
   277→{jjdt_coronet}
   278→embeds the destination
   279→{jjdt_firemark}.
   280→{jjdkr_tack}
   281→history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   282→{jjdt_coronet}.
   283→
   284→[[jjdt_silks]]
   285→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   286→{jjdt_silks}::
   287→A kebab-case display name.
   288→Used for human-readable identification of
   289→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   290→and
   291→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   292→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   293→
   294→== Records
   295→
   296→=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   297→
   298→[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   299→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   300→{jjdgr_gallops}::
   301→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   302→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   303→
   304→==== Members
   305→
   306→* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   307→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   308→{jjdgm_seed}
   309→is the next
   310→{jjdt_firemark}
   311→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   312→Incremented by
   313→{jjdo_nominate}.
   314→
   315→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   316→
   317→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   318→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   319→{jjdhr_heat}::
   320→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   321→Contains zero or more
   322→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   323→representing discrete actions.
   324→Keyed in JSON by
   325→{jjdt_firemark}.
   326→
   327→==== Members
   328→
   329→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   330→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   331→{jjdhm_firemark}
   332→is the
   333→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   334→primary key.
   335→Type:
   336→{jjdt_firemark}.
   337→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   338→
   339→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   340→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   341→{jjdhm_silks}
   342→is the
   343→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   344→display name.
   345→Type:
   346→{jjdt_silks}.
   347→
   348→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   349→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   350→{jjdhm_created}
   351→records when the
   352→{jjdhr_heat}
   353→was nominated.
   354→Format: YYMMDD.
   355→
   356→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   357→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   358→{jjdhm_status}
   359→indicates the
   360→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   361→lifecycle state.
   362→Values:
   363→{jjdhe_current}
   364→or
   365→{jjdhe_retired}.
   366→
   367→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   368→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   369→{jjdhm_order}
   370→lists
   371→{jjdt_coronet_s}
   372→in execution sequence.
   373→May be reordered via
   374→{jjdo_rail}.
   375→
   376→* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   377→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   378→{jjdhm_seed}
   379→is the next
   380→{jjdt_coronet}
   381→index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   382→Incremented by
   383→{jjdo_slate}.
   384→
   385→* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   386→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   387→{jjdhm_paddock}
   388→is the path to the paddock file containing
   389→{jjdhr_heat}
   390→context and background.
   391→Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   392→{jjdt_firemark}
   393→(without `₣` prefix).
   394→Created by
   395→{jjdo_nominate}.
   396→
   397→==== Status Values
   398→
   399→[[jjdhe_current]]
   400→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   401→{jjdhe_current}::
   402→The
   403→{jjdhr_heat}
   404→is active and accepting work.
   405→
   406→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   407→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   408→{jjdhe_retired}::
   409→The
   410→{jjdhr_heat}
   411→is complete and archived.
   412→
   413→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   414→
   415→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   416→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   417→{jjdpr_pace}::
   418→A discrete action within a
   419→{jjdhr_heat}.
   420→Contains one or more
   421→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   422→representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   423→Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   424→Keyed in JSON by
   425→{jjdt_coronet}.
   426→
   427→==== Members
   428→
   429→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   430→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   431→{jjdpm_coronet}
   432→is the
   433→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   434→globally unique identity.
   435→Type:
   436→{jjdt_coronet}.
   437→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   438→
   439→* [[jjdpm_silks]]
   440→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   441→{jjdpm_silks}
   442→is the
   443→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   444→display name.
   445→Type:
   446→{jjdt_silks}.
   447→
   448→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   449→
   450→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   451→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   452→{jjdkr_tack}::
   453→A snapshot of
   454→{jjdpr_pace}
   455→state and plan at a point in time.
   456→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   457→form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   458→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   459→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   460→follow.
   461→New
   462→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   463→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   464→The current
   465→{jjdpr_pace}
   466→state is always
   467→`tacks[0].state`.
   468→
   469→==== Members
   470→
   471→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   472→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   473→{jjdkm_refined}
   474→records when this
   475→{jjdkr_tack}
   476→was added.
   477→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   478→
   479→* [[jjdkm_state]]
   480→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   481→{jjdkm_state}
   482→indicates the
   483→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   484→progress at this snapshot.
   485→Values:
   486→{jjdpe_rough},
   487→{jjdpe_primed},
   488→{jjdpe_complete},
   489→or
   490→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   491→
   492→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   493→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   494→{jjdkm_text}
   495→contains the plan specification.
   496→Non-empty string describing what the
   497→{jjdpr_pace}
   498→should accomplish.
   499→
   500→* [[jjdkm_direction]]
   501→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional⟧
   502→{jjdkm_direction}
   503→contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   504→Required when
   505→{jjdkm_state}
   506→is
   507→{jjdpe_primed};
   508→forbidden otherwise.
   509→Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.
   510→
   511→==== State Values
   512→
   513→[[jjdpe_rough]]
   514→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   515→{jjdpe_rough}::
   516→The
   517→{jjdpr_pace}
   518→needs clarification before work can proceed.
   519→When context is assembled for a
   520→{jjdpe_rough}
   521→pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.
   522→
   523→[[jjdpe_primed]]
   524→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   525→{jjdpe_primed}::
   526→The
   527→{jjdpr_pace}
   528→is ready for autonomous execution.
   529→The
   530→{jjdkm_direction}
   531→field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
   532→When context is assembled for a
   533→{jjdpe_primed}
   534→pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.
   535→
   536→[[jjdpe_complete]]
   537→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   538→{jjdpe_complete}::
   539→The
   540→{jjdpr_pace}
   541→has been successfully finished.
   542→
   543→[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
   544→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   545→{jjdpe_abandoned}::
   546→The
   547→{jjdpr_pace}
   548→was stopped without completion.
   549→
   550→== Serialization
   551→
   552→[[jjdz_charset]]
   553→{jjdz_charset}::
   554→The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   555→Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
   556→`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   557→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   558→This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   559→Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   560→
   561→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   562→{jjdz_encoding}::
   563→The algorithm for encoding
   564→{jjdt_firemark}
   565→and
   566→{jjdt_coronet}
   567→values.
   568→+
   569→*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   570→+
   571→. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
   572→. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   573→. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   574→+
   575→*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   576→+
   577→. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
   578→. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   579→. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   580→. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   581→. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   582→+
   583→*Input flexibility:*
   584→+
   585→CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   586→Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   587→Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).
   588→
   589→== Arguments
   590→
   591→*Note:* {jjda_file} appears as the first argument on all operations.
   592→It is annotated `// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧` and uses the default value when not explicitly provided.
   593→
   594→[[jjda_file]]
   595→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   596→{jjda_file}::
   597→Path to the
   598→{jjdgr_gallops}
   599→JSON file.
   600→Long: `--file`.
   601→Short: none.
   602→Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   603→
   604→[[jjda_silks]]
   605→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   606→{jjda_silks}::
   607→Kebab-case display name for the target entity.
   608→Long: `--silks`.
   609→Short: `-s`.
   610→
   611→[[jjda_state]]
   612→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   613→{jjda_state}::
   614→Target state for the
   615→{jjdkr_tack}.
   616→Long: `--state`.
   617→Short: none.
   618→Values:
   619→{jjdpe_rough},
   620→{jjdpe_primed},
   621→{jjdpe_complete},
   622→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   623→
   624→[[jjda_direction]]
   625→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   626→{jjda_direction}::
   627→Execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   628→Long: `--direction`.
   629→Short: `-d`.
   630→Required when
   631→{jjda_state}
   632→is
   633→{jjdpe_primed};
   634→forbidden otherwise.
   635→
   636→[[jjda_pace]]
   637→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   638→{jjda_pace}::
   639→Pace silks for context in commit messages.
   640→Long: `--pace`.
   641→Short: `-p`.
   642→
   643→[[jjda_created]]
   644→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   645→{jjda_created}::
   646→Creation date in YYMMDD format.
   647→Long: `--created`.
   648→Short: `-c`.
   649→
   650→[[jjda_before]]
   651→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   652→{jjda_before}::
   653→{jjdt_coronet}
   654→to insert before.
   655→Long: `--before`.
   656→Short: none.
   657→Mutually exclusive with
   658→{jjda_after}
   659→and
   660→{jjda_first}.
   661→
   662→[[jjda_after]]
   663→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   664→{jjda_after}::
   665→{jjdt_coronet}
   666→to insert after.
   667→Long: `--after`.
   668→Short: none.
   669→Mutually exclusive with
   670→{jjda_before}
   671→and
   672→{jjda_first}.
   673→
   674→[[jjda_first]]
   675→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_flag⟧
   676→{jjda_first}::
   677→Position at beginning of
   678→{jjdhm_order}.
   679→Long: `--first`.
   680→Short: none.
   681→Boolean flag, no value.
   682→Mutually exclusive with
   683→{jjda_before}
   684→and
   685→{jjda_after}.
   686→
   687→[[jjda_last]]
   688→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_flag⟧
   689→{jjda_last}::
   690→Move to end of
   691→{jjdhm_order}.
   692→Long: `--last`.
   693→Short: none.
   694→Boolean flag, no value.
   695→Used only by
   696→{jjdo_rail}
   697→move mode.
   698→Mutually exclusive with
   699→{jjda_before},
   700→{jjda_after},
   701→and
   702→{jjda_first}.
   703→
   704→[[jjda_move]]
   705→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   706→{jjda_move}::
   707→{jjdt_coronet}
   708→to relocate within
   709→{jjdhm_order}.
   710→Long: `--move`.
   711→Short: `-m`.
   712→Triggers move mode in
   713→{jjdo_rail}.
   714→Requires exactly one positioning flag:
   715→{jjda_before},
   716→{jjda_after},
   717→{jjda_first},
   718→or
   719→{jjda_last}.
   720→
   721→[[jjda_status]]
   722→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   723→{jjda_status}::
   724→Filter by
   725→{jjdhr_heat}
   726→status.
   727→Long: `--status`.
   728→Short: none.
   729→Values:
   730→{jjdhe_current},
   731→{jjdhe_retired}.
   732→
   733→[[jjda_to]]
   734→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
   735→{jjda_to}::
   736→Destination
   737→{jjdt_firemark}
   738→for
   739→{jjdo_draft}.
   740→Long: `--to`.

   743→== Section Headers
   744→
   745→[[jjds_arguments]]
   746→// ⟦axl_voices axa_argument_list⟧
   747→{jjds_arguments}::

   755→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_uniform⟧

   767→
   768→== CLI
   769→
   770→[[jjdx_vvx]]
   771→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_program⟧
   772→{jjdx_vvx}::
   773→The Voce Viva executor.
   774→Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.
   775→
   776→[[jjdx_cli]]
   777→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_command_group⟧
   778→{jjdx_cli}::
   779→The Job Jockey command group within
   780→{jjdx_vvx}.
   781→Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
   782→All operations are subcommands of
   783→{jjdx_cli}.
   784→Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
   785→
   786→== Operations
   787→
   788→Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
   789→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   790→{jjdgr_gallops}
   791→file access (read, transform, atomic write).
   792→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
   793→{jjda_file}.
   794→
   795→=== Write Operations
   796→
   797→[[jjdo_nominate]]
   798→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
   799→==== {jjdo_nominate}
   800→
   801→Create a new
   802→{jjdhr_heat}
   803→with empty
   804→{jjdpr_pace}
   805→structure.
   806→
   807→{jjds_arguments}
   808→
   809→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
   810→* {jjda_file}
   811→
   812→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   813→* {jjda_silks}
   814→
   815→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   816→* {jjda_created}
   817→
   818→{jjds_stdout} New
   819→{jjdt_firemark}
   820→
   821→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   822→
   823→{jjds_behavior}
   824→
   825→. If
   826→{jjdgr_gallops}
   827→file does not exist:
   828→.. Create parent directory `.claude/jjm/` if needed
   829→.. Initialize new
   830→{jjdgr_gallops}
   831→with
   832→{jjdgm_seed}
   833→`AA` and empty `heats`
   834→. Read
   835→{jjdgr_gallops}
   836→from file (or use newly created)
   837→. Read current
   838→{jjdgm_seed}
   839→as
   840→{jjdt_firemark}
   841→. Compute paddock path: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the 2 base64 chars
   842→. Create paddock file with template:
   843→+
   844→----
   845→# Paddock: {silks}
   846→
   847→## Context
   848→
   849→(Describe the initiative's background and goals)
   850→
   851→## References
   852→
   853→(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)
   854→----
   855→. Create new
   856→{jjdhr_heat}
   857→with:
   858→.. Key: `₣` + encoded
   859→{jjdt_firemark}
   860→.. {jjdhm_silks}:
   861→from argument
   862→.. {jjdhm_created}:
   863→from argument
   864→.. {jjdhm_status}:
   865→{jjdhe_current}
   866→.. {jjdhm_order}:
   867→empty array
   868→.. {jjdhm_seed}:
   869→`AAA` (index 0)
   870→.. {jjdhm_paddock}:
   871→computed paddock path
   872→.. `paces`: empty object
   873→. Increment
   874→{jjdgm_seed}
   875→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
   876→. Write transformed
   877→{jjdgr_gallops}
   878→to file (atomic)
   879→. Output new
   880→{jjdt_firemark}
   881→to stdout
   882→
   883→[[jjdo_slate]]
   884→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
   885→==== {jjdo_slate}
   886→
   887→Add a new
   888→{jjdpr_pace}
   889→to a
   890→{jjdhr_heat}.
   891→The initial
   892→{jjdkr_tack}
   893→is created with state
   894→{jjdpe_rough}.
   895→
   896→By default, the new
   897→{jjdpr_pace}
   898→is appended to the end of
   899→{jjdhm_order}.
   900→Use positioning flags to insert at a specific location.
   901→
   902→{jjds_arguments}
   903→
   904→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
   905→* {jjda_file}
   906→
   907→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   908→* {jjdt_firemark}
   909→(positional) — target
   910→{jjdhr_heat}
   911→
   912→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   913→* {jjda_silks}
   914→
   915→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   916→* stdin —
   917→{jjdkm_text}
   918→content for the initial
   919→{jjdkr_tack}
   920→
   921→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   922→* {jjda_before}
   923→— insert before specified
   924→{jjdt_coronet}
   925→
   926→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   927→* {jjda_after}
   928→— insert after specified
   929→{jjdt_coronet}
   930→
   931→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
   932→* {jjda_first}
   933→— insert at beginning of
   934→{jjdhm_order}
   935→
   936→*Positioning:* Only one of
   937→{jjda_before},
   938→{jjda_after},
   939→or
   940→{jjda_first}
   941→may be specified.
   942→If none provided, append to end (default).
   943→Note:
   944→{jjdo_rail}
   945→reorders existing
   946→{jjdpr_pace_s};
   947→{jjdo_slate}
   948→positioning inserts a new
   949→{jjdpr_pace}
   950→at a specific location.
   951→
   952→{jjds_stdout} New
   953→{jjdt_coronet}
   954→
   955→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   956→
   957→{jjds_behavior}
   958→
   959→. Read
   960→{jjdgr_gallops}
   961→from file
   962→. Verify
   963→{jjdhr_heat}
   964→exists with given
   965→{jjdt_firemark}
   966→. If
   967→{jjda_before}
   968→or
   969→{jjda_after}
   970→provided, verify target
   971→{jjdt_coronet}
   972→exists in
   973→{jjdhr_heat}
   974→. Read
   975→{jjdhm_seed}
   976→from target
   977→{jjdhr_heat}
   978→. Construct
   979→{jjdt_coronet}:
   980→`₢` + encoded
   981→{jjdt_firemark}
   982→(2 chars) +
   983→{jjdhm_seed}
   984→(3 chars)
   985→. Create new
   986→{jjdpr_pace}
   987→with:
   988→.. Key: constructed
   989→{jjdt_coronet}
   990→.. {jjdpm_silks}:
   991→from argument
   992→.. `tacks`: array with single
   993→{jjdkr_tack}:
   994→... {jjdkm_refined}:
   995→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
   996→... {jjdkm_state}:
   997→{jjdpe_rough}
   998→... {jjdkm_text}:
   999→from stdin
  1000→. Insert
  1001→{jjdt_coronet}
  1002→into
  1003→{jjdhm_order}
  1004→at determined position:
  1005→.. If
  1006→{jjda_first}:
  1007→prepend to beginning
  1008→.. If
  1009→{jjda_before}:
  1010→insert immediately before target
  1011→{jjdt_coronet}
  1012→.. If
  1013→{jjda_after}:
  1014→insert immediately after target
  1015→{jjdt_coronet}
  1016→.. Otherwise: append to end
  1017→. Increment
  1018→{jjdhm_seed}
  1019→(base64 arithmetic with carry)
  1020→. Write transformed
  1021→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1022→to file (atomic)
  1023→. Output new
  1024→{jjdt_coronet}
  1025→to stdout
  1026→
  1027→[[jjdo_rail]]
  1028→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1029→==== {jjdo_rail}
  1030→
  1031→Reorder
  1032→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1033→within a
  1034→{jjdhr_heat}
  1035→by updating
  1036→{jjdhm_order}.
  1037→Supports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
  1038→{jjdpr_pace}.
  1039→
  1040→*Order mode* (full replacement):
  1041→----
  1042→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> <CORONET>...
  1043→----
  1044→
  1045→*Move mode* (single relocation):
  1046→----
  1047→vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> --move <CORONET> [--before <TARGET> | --after <TARGET> | --first | --last]
  1048→----
  1049→
  1050→{jjds_arguments}
  1051→
  1052→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1053→* {jjda_file}
  1054→
  1055→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1056→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1057→(positional) — target
  1058→{jjdhr_heat}
  1059→
  1060→*Order mode arguments:*
  1061→
  1062→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1063→* order — array of
  1064→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1065→in desired sequence (space-separated positional arguments)
  1066→
  1067→*Move mode arguments:*
  1068→
  1069→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1070→* {jjda_move}
  1071→— {jjdt_coronet}
  1072→to relocate
  1073→
  1074→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1075→* Exactly one positioning flag:
  1076→{jjda_before},
  1077→{jjda_after},
  1078→{jjda_first},
  1079→or
  1080→{jjda_last}
  1081→
  1082→*Mode detection:* If
  1083→{jjda_move}
  1084→is present, move mode; otherwise order mode.
  1085→
  1086→{jjds_stdout} Updated
  1087→{jjdhm_order}
  1088→array, one
  1089→{jjdt_coronet}
  1090→per line, in new sequence.
  1091→
  1092→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1093→
  1094→{jjds_behavior}
  1095→
  1096→*Common steps:*
  1097→
  1098→. Read
  1099→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1100→from file
  1101→. Verify
  1102→{jjdhr_heat}
  1103→exists with given
  1104→{jjdt_firemark}
  1105→
  1106→*Order mode:*
  1107→
  1108→. Parse positional arguments into array of
  1109→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1110→. Validate order array:
  1111→.. Count must match current
  1112→{jjdhm_order}
  1113→length
  1114→.. No duplicate
  1115→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1116→.. All
  1117→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1118→must exist in
  1119→{jjdhr_heat_p}
  1120→`paces` object
  1121→.. All
  1122→{jjdt_coronet_s}
  1123→must embed correct parent
  1124→{jjdt_firemark}
  1125→. Replace
  1126→{jjdhm_order}
  1127→with new order array
  1128→. Write transformed
  1129→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1130→to file (atomic)
  1131→. Output new
  1132→{jjdhm_order},
  1133→one
  1134→{jjdt_coronet}
  1135→per line
  1136→
  1137→*Move mode:*
  1138→
  1139→. Validate
  1140→{jjda_move}
  1141→{jjdt_coronet}
  1142→exists in
  1143→{jjdhr_heat}
  1144→. Validate exactly one positioning flag provided
  1145→. If
  1146→{jjda_before}
  1147→or
  1148→{jjda_after}:
  1149→validate target
  1150→{jjdt_coronet}
  1151→exists and differs from
  1152→{jjda_move}
  1153→{jjdt_coronet}
  1154→. Remove
  1155→{jjda_move}
  1156→{jjdt_coronet}
  1157→from current position in
  1158→{jjdhm_order}
  1159→. Insert at new position:
  1160→.. If
  1161→{jjda_first}:
  1162→prepend to beginning
  1163→.. If
  1164→{jjda_last}:
  1165→append to end
  1166→.. If
  1167→{jjda_before}:
  1168→insert immediately before target
  1169→{jjdt_coronet}
  1170→.. If
  1171→{jjda_after}:
  1172→insert immediately after target
  1173→{jjdt_coronet}
  1174→. Write transformed
  1175→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1176→to file (atomic)
  1177→. Output new
  1178→{jjdhm_order},
  1179→one
  1180→{jjdt_coronet}
  1181→per line
  1182→
  1183→*Validation errors:*
  1184→
  1185→[cols="2,3"]
  1186→|===
  1187→| Condition | Error
  1188→
  1189→| {jjda_move} with positional coronets
  1190→| "Cannot combine --move with positional coronets"
  1191→
  1192→| {jjda_move} {jjdt_coronet} not found
  1193→| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1194→
  1195→| No positioning flag provided
  1196→| "Move mode requires exactly one positioning flag"
  1197→
  1198→| Multiple positioning flags
  1199→| "Conflicting positioning flags: {flags}"
  1200→
  1201→| Target same as move
  1202→| "Cannot position pace relative to itself"
  1203→
  1204→| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found
  1205→| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1206→|===
  1207→
  1208→[[jjdo_draft]]
  1209→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1210→==== {jjdo_draft}
  1211→
  1212→Move a
  1213→{jjdpr_pace}
  1214→from one
  1215→{jjdhr_heat}
  1216→to another.
  1217→This is a primitive operation: mechanical transfer with no ceremony.
  1218→The
  1219→{jjdpr_pace}
  1220→receives a new
  1221→{jjdt_coronet}
  1222→in the destination
  1223→{jjdhr_heat};
  1224→all
  1225→{jjdkr_tack}
  1226→history is preserved.
  1227→
  1228→*Important:* "Drafted" is not a
  1229→{jjdpr_pace}
  1230→state.
  1231→{jjdpr_pace}
  1232→states remain:
  1233→{jjdpe_rough},
  1234→{jjdpe_primed},
  1235→{jjdpe_complete},
  1236→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
  1237→Draft is an operation that moves
  1238→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1239→between
  1240→{jjdhr_heat_s};
  1241→state is preserved through the move.
  1242→
  1243→{jjds_arguments}
  1244→
  1245→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1246→* {jjda_file}
  1247→
  1248→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1249→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1250→(positional) —
  1251→{jjdpr_pace}
  1252→to move
  1253→
  1254→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1255→* {jjda_to}
  1256→— destination
  1257→{jjdt_firemark}
  1258→
  1259→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1260→* {jjda_before}
  1261→— insert before specified
  1262→{jjdt_coronet}
  1263→in destination
  1264→
  1265→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1266→* {jjda_after}
  1267→— insert after specified
  1268→{jjdt_coronet}
  1269→in destination
  1270→
  1271→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1272→* {jjda_first}
  1273→— insert at beginning of destination
  1274→{jjdhm_order}
  1275→
  1276→*Positioning:* Only one of
  1277→{jjda_before},
  1278→{jjda_after},
  1279→or
  1280→{jjda_first}
  1281→may be specified.
  1282→If none provided, append to end of destination
  1283→{jjdhm_order}
  1284→(default).
  1285→
  1286→{jjds_stdout} New
  1287→{jjdt_coronet}
  1288→in destination
  1289→{jjdhr_heat}
  1290→
  1291→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1292→
  1293→{jjds_behavior}
  1294→
  1295→. Read
  1296→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1297→from file
  1298→. Extract source
  1299→{jjdt_firemark}
  1300→from
  1301→{jjdt_coronet}
  1302→(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1303→. Verify source
  1304→{jjdhr_heat}
  1305→exists
  1306→. Verify
  1307→{jjdpr_pace}
  1308→exists in source
  1309→{jjdhr_heat}
  1310→. Verify destination
  1311→{jjdhr_heat}
  1312→exists with given
  1313→{jjdt_firemark}
  1314→. If
  1315→{jjda_before}
  1316→or
  1317→{jjda_after}
  1318→provided, verify target
  1319→{jjdt_coronet}
  1320→exists in destination
  1321→{jjdhr_heat}
  1322→. Read destination
  1323→{jjdhm_seed}
  1324→. Construct new
  1325→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1326→`₢` + destination
  1327→{jjdt_firemark}
  1328→(2 chars) + destination
  1329→{jjdhm_seed}
  1330→(3 chars)
  1331→. Copy
  1332→{jjdpr_pace}
  1333→data to destination with new
  1334→{jjdt_coronet}:
  1335→.. Preserve
  1336→{jjdpm_silks}
  1337→.. Prepend new
  1338→{jjdkr_tack}
  1339→recording the draft:
  1340→... {jjdkm_refined}:
  1341→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1342→... {jjdkm_state}:
  1343→inherited from previous `tacks[0].state`
  1344→... {jjdkm_text}:
  1345→`Drafted from {old_coronet} in {source_firemark}.\n\n` + previous `tacks[0].text`
  1346→.. Preserve remaining
  1347→{jjdkr_tack}
  1348→history
  1349→. Insert new
  1350→{jjdt_coronet}
  1351→into destination
  1352→{jjdhm_order}
  1353→at determined position
  1354→. Increment destination
  1355→{jjdhm_seed}
  1356→. Remove
  1357→{jjdpr_pace}
  1358→from source
  1359→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1360→.. Remove from source `paces` object
  1361→.. Remove from source
  1362→{jjdhm_order}
  1363→. Write transformed
  1364→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1365→to file (atomic)
  1366→. Output new
  1367→{jjdt_coronet}
  1368→to stdout
  1369→
  1370→*Validation errors:*
  1371→
  1372→[cols="2,3"]
  1373→|===
  1374→| Condition | Error
  1375→
  1376→| Source and destination same
  1377→| "Cannot draft pace to same heat"
  1378→
  1379→| Source {jjdpr_pace} not found
  1380→| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1381→
  1382→| Destination {jjdhr_heat} not found
  1383→| "Heat {firemark} not found"
  1384→
  1385→| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found in destination
  1386→| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
  1387→|===
  1388→
  1389→[[jjdo_tally]]
  1390→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1391→==== {jjdo_tally}
  1392→
  1393→Add a new
  1394→{jjdkr_tack}
  1395→to a
  1396→{jjdpr_pace}.
  1397→This is the unified operation for all
  1398→{jjdkr_tack}
  1399→creation: state transitions, plan refinements, or both.
  1400→
  1401→{jjds_arguments}
  1402→
  1403→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1404→* {jjda_file}
  1405→
  1406→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1407→* {jjdt_coronet}
  1408→(positional)
  1409→
  1410→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1411→* {jjda_state}
  1412→— if absent, inherits from previous
  1413→{jjdkr_tack}
  1414→
  1415→// ⟦axd_conditional⟧
  1416→* {jjda_direction}
  1417→— required if
  1418→{jjda_state}
  1419→is
  1420→{jjdpe_primed};
  1421→forbidden if
  1422→{jjda_state}
  1423→is provided and not
  1424→{jjdpe_primed};
  1425→ignored if
  1426→{jjda_state}
  1427→is absent (inherits with state)
  1428→
  1429→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1430→* stdin —
  1431→{jjdkm_text}
  1432→content; if absent, inherits from previous
  1433→{jjdkr_tack}
  1434→
  1435→{jjds_stdout} None
  1436→
  1437→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1438→
  1439→{jjds_behavior}
  1440→
  1441→. Read
  1442→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1443→from file
  1444→. Extract parent
  1445→{jjdt_firemark}
  1446→from
  1447→{jjdt_coronet}
  1448→(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1449→. Verify
  1450→{jjdhr_heat}
  1451→exists
  1452→. Verify
  1453→{jjdpr_pace}
  1454→exists with given
  1455→{jjdt_coronet}
  1456→. Read current
  1457→{jjdkr_tack}
  1458→from `tacks[0]`
  1459→. Determine new state:
  1460→.. If
  1461→{jjda_state}
  1462→provided: use that value
  1463→.. Otherwise: inherit from previous
  1464→{jjdkr_tack}
  1465→. Determine new direction:
  1466→.. If
  1467→{jjda_state}
  1468→is
  1469→{jjdpe_primed}:
  1470→use
  1471→{jjda_direction}
  1472→argument (required)
  1473→.. If
  1474→{jjda_state}
  1475→is absent and previous state is
  1476→{jjdpe_primed}:
  1477→inherit from previous
  1478→{jjdkr_tack}
  1479→.. Otherwise: omit
  1480→{jjdkm_direction}
  1481→. Determine new text:
  1482→.. If stdin provided: use stdin content
  1483→.. Otherwise: inherit from previous
  1484→{jjdkr_tack}
  1485→. Create new
  1486→{jjdkr_tack}
  1487→with:
  1488→.. {jjdkm_refined}:
  1489→current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1490→.. {jjdkm_state}:
  1491→determined above
  1492→.. {jjdkm_text}:
  1493→determined above
  1494→.. {jjdkm_direction}:
  1495→determined above (if applicable)
  1496→. Prepend new
  1497→{jjdkr_tack}
  1498→to `tacks` array (position 0)
  1499→. Write transformed
  1500→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1501→to file (atomic)
  1502→
  1503→[[jjdo_retire]]
  1504→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1505→==== {jjdo_retire}
  1506→
  1507→Archive a
  1508→{jjdhr_heat}
  1509→by writing a trophy file, removing the heat from
  1510→{jjdgr_gallops},
  1511→deleting the paddock file, and committing.
  1512→This is a destructive operation that permanently removes the heat from active tracking.
  1513→
  1514→{jjds_arguments}
  1515→
  1516→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1517→* {jjda_file}
  1518→
  1519→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1520→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1521→(positional) — target
  1522→{jjdhr_heat}
  1523→
  1524→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1525→* `--execute` — perform the retire operation.
  1526→Without this flag, outputs trophy markdown to stdout as a dry-run preview.
  1527→
  1528→{jjds_stdout}
  1529→Without `--execute`: trophy markdown content (dry-run preview).
  1530→With `--execute`: path to the written trophy file.
  1531→
  1532→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1533→
  1534→{jjds_behavior}
  1535→
  1536→Without `--execute` (dry-run):
  1537→
  1538→. Read
  1539→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1540→from file
  1541→. Verify
  1542→{jjdhr_heat}
  1543→exists with given
  1544→{jjdt_firemark}
  1545→. Read paddock file contents from
  1546→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1547→. Call
  1548→{jjdo_rein}
  1549→internally to get steeplechase entries
  1550→. Build trophy markdown (see format below)
  1551→. Output trophy markdown to stdout
  1552→
  1553→With `--execute`:
  1554→
  1555→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1556→. Read
  1557→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1558→from file
  1559→. Verify
  1560→{jjdhr_heat}
  1561→exists with given
  1562→{jjdt_firemark}
  1563→. Extract
  1564→{jjdhr_heat}
  1565→metadata:
  1566→{jjdhm_silks},
  1567→{jjdhm_created}
  1568→. Read paddock file contents from
  1569→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1570→. Call
  1571→{jjdo_rein}
  1572→internally to get steeplechase entries
  1573→. Build trophy markdown:
  1574→+
  1575→----
  1576→# Heat Trophy: {silks}
  1577→
  1578→**Firemark:** {firemark}
  1579→**Created:** {created}
  1580→**Retired:** {today}
  1581→**Status:** retired
  1582→
  1583→## Paddock
  1584→
  1585→{full paddock content}
  1586→
  1587→## Paces
  1588→
  1589→{for each pace in order:}
  1590→
  1591→### {pace_silks} ({coronet}) [{final_state}]
  1592→
  1593→{for each tack, newest first:}
  1594→**[{ts}] {state}**
  1595→
  1596→{tack text}
  1597→
  1598→{if tack has direction:}
  1599→*Direction:* {direction}
  1600→
  1601→## Steeplechase
  1602→
  1603→{for each steeplechase entry from rein, newest first:}
  1604→
  1605→### {date} - {pace_silks or "Heat"} - {marker or "commit"}
  1606→
  1607→{subject/description}
  1608→
  1609→----
  1610→. Compute trophy filename: `.claude/jjm/retired/jjh_b{created}-r{today}-{silks}.md`
  1611→where `{today}` is current date in YYMMDD format
  1612→. Create `.claude/jjm/retired/` directory if it does not exist
  1613→. Write trophy markdown to trophy file
  1614→. Remove
  1615→{jjdhr_heat}
  1616→entry from
  1617→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1618→`heats` object
  1619→. Write transformed
  1620→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1621→to file (atomic)
  1622→. Delete paddock file at
  1623→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1624→. Stage exactly three paths: trophy file, gallops.json, paddock file (as deletion)
  1625→. Run size guard with 200KB limit (trophy files can be large for big heats)
  1626→. Commit with message per steeplechase conventions (format TBD by unify-commit-format)
  1627→. Release lock
  1628→. Output trophy file path to stdout
  1629→
  1630→=== Read Operations
  1631→
  1632→[[jjdo_validate]]
  1633→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1634→==== {jjdo_validate}
  1635→
  1636→Check that
  1637→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1638→JSON conforms to schema.
  1639→
  1640→{jjds_arguments}
  1641→
  1642→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1643→* {jjda_file}
  1644→
  1645→{jjds_stdout} None
  1646→
  1647→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1648→
  1649→*Validation Rules:*
  1650→
  1651→. Root must be JSON object with
  1652→{jjdgm_seed}
  1653→(2 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`)
  1654→. Root must have `heats` object
  1655→. Each
  1656→{jjdhr_heat}
  1657→key must match `₣[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`
  1658→. Each
  1659→{jjdhr_heat}
  1660→must have:
  1661→{jjdhm_silks}
  1662→(non-empty kebab-case),
  1663→{jjdhm_created}
  1664→(YYMMDD),
  1665→{jjdhm_status}
  1666→({jjdhe_current}
  1667→or
  1668→{jjdhe_retired}),
  1669→{jjdhm_order}
  1670→(array),
  1671→{jjdhm_seed}
  1672→(3 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{3}`),
  1673→`paces` (object)
  1674→. {jjdhm_order}
  1675→array and `paces` object must have identical key sets
  1676→. Each
  1677→{jjdpr_pace}
  1678→key must match `₢[A-Za-z0-9_-]{5}` and embed parent
  1679→{jjdhr_heat}
  1680→identity (first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1681→. Each
  1682→{jjdpr_pace}
  1683→must have:
  1684→{jjdpm_silks}
  1685→(non-empty kebab-case),
  1686→`tacks` (non-empty array)
  1687→. Each
  1688→{jjdkr_tack}
  1689→must have:
  1690→{jjdkm_refined}
  1691→(YYMMDD-HHMM),
  1692→{jjdkm_state}
  1693→({jjdpe_rough},
  1694→{jjdpe_primed},
  1695→{jjdpe_complete},
  1696→or
  1697→{jjdpe_abandoned}),
  1698→{jjdkm_text}
  1699→(non-empty string)
  1700→. If
  1701→{jjdkm_state}
  1702→is
  1703→{jjdpe_primed},
  1704→{jjdkm_direction}
  1705→must be present (non-empty string); otherwise
  1706→{jjdkm_direction}
  1707→must be absent
  1708→
  1709→[[jjdo_muster]]
  1710→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1711→==== {jjdo_muster}
  1712→
  1713→List all
  1714→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1715→with summary information.
  1716→
  1717→{jjds_arguments}
  1718→
  1719→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1720→* {jjda_file}
  1721→
  1722→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1723→* {jjda_status}
  1724→— filter to show only
  1725→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1726→with this status; if absent, show all
  1727→
  1728→{jjds_stdout} Tab-separated values, one line per
  1729→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1730→`{jjdt_firemark}<TAB>{jjdt_silks}<TAB>{jjdhm_status}<TAB><pace_count>`
  1731→
  1732→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1733→
  1734→{jjds_behavior}
  1735→
  1736→. Read
  1737→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1738→from file
  1739→. Iterate over `heats` object entries
  1740→. If
  1741→{jjda_status}
  1742→provided, skip
  1743→{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1744→not matching filter
  1745→. For each included
  1746→{jjdhr_heat},
  1747→output TSV line:
  1748→{jjdt_firemark},
  1749→{jjdhm_silks},
  1750→{jjdhm_status},
  1751→count of
  1752→{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1753→in `paces` object
  1754→. Output lines in arbitrary order (JSON object iteration order)
  1755→
  1756→[[jjdo_saddle]]
  1757→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1758→==== {jjdo_saddle}
  1759→
  1760→Return context needed to saddle up on a
  1761→{jjdhr_heat}:
  1762→the current
  1763→{jjdpr_pace}
  1764→(first in
  1765→{jjdhm_order}
  1766→whose state is
  1767→{jjdpe_rough}
  1768→or
  1769→{jjdpe_primed})
  1770→and its
  1771→{jjdkr_tack}
  1772→details.
  1773→
  1774→{jjds_arguments}
  1775→
  1776→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1777→* {jjda_file}
  1778→
  1779→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1780→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1781→(positional) — target
  1782→{jjdhr_heat}
  1783→
  1784→{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:
  1785→[source,json]
  1786→----
  1787→{
  1788→  "heat_silks": "...",
  1789→  "paddock_file": ".claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md",
  1790→  "paddock_content": "...",
  1791→  "pace_coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  1792→  "pace_silks": "...",
  1793→  "pace_state": "rough|primed",
  1794→  "tack_text": "...",
  1795→  "tack_direction": "..."
  1796→}
  1797→----
  1798→Fields `pace_coronet` through `tack_direction` are absent if no actionable pace exists.
  1799→Field `tack_direction` is present only when `pace_state` is
  1800→{jjdpe_primed}.
  1801→Field `paddock_content` contains the full text of the paddock file.
  1802→
  1803→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1804→
  1805→{jjds_behavior}
  1806→
  1807→. Read
  1808→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1809→from file
  1810→. Verify
  1811→{jjdhr_heat}
  1812→exists with given
  1813→{jjdt_firemark}
  1814→. Output `heat_silks` from
  1815→{jjdhr_heat}
  1816→. Output `paddock_file` from
  1817→{jjdhm_paddock}
  1818→. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
  1819→. Walk
  1820→{jjdhm_order}
  1821→array in sequence
  1822→. For each
  1823→{jjdt_coronet},
  1824→check `paces[coronet].tacks[0].state`
  1825→. If state is
  1826→{jjdpe_rough}
  1827→or
  1828→{jjdpe_primed}:
  1829→.. Output `pace_coronet`,
  1830→`pace_silks`,
  1831→`pace_state`
  1832→.. Output `tack_text` from `tacks[0].text`
  1833→.. If state is
  1834→{jjdpe_primed},
  1835→output `tack_direction` from `tacks[0].direction`
  1836→.. Stop walking (first match wins)
  1837→. If no actionable pace found, output only `heat_silks`, `paddock_file`, and `paddock_content`
  1838→
  1839→[[jjdo_parade]]
  1840→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1841→==== {jjdo_parade}
  1842→
  1843→Display
  1844→{jjdhr_heat}
  1845→status in human-readable text format.
  1846→Output varies by format mode.
  1847→
  1848→{jjds_arguments}
  1849→
  1850→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1851→* {jjda_file}
  1852→
  1853→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1854→* {jjdt_firemark}
  1855→(positional) — target
  1856→{jjdhr_heat}
  1857→
  1858→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1859→* `--format <mode>` — output format mode (default: `full`)
  1860→
  1861→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  1862→* `--pace <coronet>` — target
  1863→{jjdt_coronet}
  1864→(required with `--format detail`)
  1865→
  1866→*Format Modes*
  1867→
  1868→[cols="1,2,3"]
  1869→|===
  1870→| Mode | Purpose | Output
  1871→
  1872→| `overview`
  1873→| Quick status
  1874→| One line per pace: `[state] silks (₢coronet)`
  1875→
  1876→| `order`
  1877→| Dependency check
  1878→| Numbered: `N. [state] silks (₢coronet)`
  1879→
  1880→| `detail`
  1881→| Inspect one pace
  1882→| Full tack text for specified pace (requires `--pace`)
  1883→
  1884→| `full`
  1885→| Planning context
  1886→| Paddock content + all paces with tack text
  1887→|===
  1888→
  1889→{jjds_stdout} Human-readable text (not JSON).
  1890→
  1891→*Example output — overview:*
  1892→----
  1893→[abandoned] vvw-workbench-tabtarget (₢AAAAC)
  1894→[complete] install-arch-decision (₢AAAAI)
  1895→[rough] vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1896→----
  1897→
  1898→*Example output — order:*
  1899→----
  1900→1. [abandoned] vvw-workbench-tabtarget (₢AAAAC)
  1901→2. [complete] install-arch-decision (₢AAAAI)
  1902→3. [rough] vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1903→----
  1904→
  1905→*Example output — detail:*
  1906→----
  1907→Pace: vvx-push-rename (₢AAAAJ)
  1908→State: rough
  1909→Heat: ₣AA
  1910→
  1911→[full tack text...]
  1912→----
  1913→
  1914→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1915→
  1916→{jjds_behavior}
  1917→
  1918→. Validate arguments (if `--format detail`, require `--pace`)
  1919→. Read
  1920→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1921→from file
  1922→. Verify
  1923→{jjdhr_heat}
  1924→exists with given
  1925→{jjdt_firemark}
  1926→. Match on format mode:
  1927→.. *overview*: For each pace in
  1928→{jjdhm_order},
  1929→print `[state] silks (coronet)`
  1930→.. *order*: Same as overview with line numbers
  1931→.. *detail*: Find specified pace, print header and full tack text
  1932→.. *full*: Print heat header, paddock content, then each pace with tack text
  1933→
  1934→=== Steeplechase Operations
  1935→
  1936→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1937→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1938→JSON.
  1939→Commit messages follow patterns that
  1940→{jjdo_rein}
  1941→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  1942→
  1943→==== Commit Message Patterns
  1944→
  1945→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY[:ACTION]: message`
  1946→
  1947→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  1948→* `BRAND` — repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time (e.g., "RBM")
  1949→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
  1950→* `ACTION` — single-letter code (optional for standard notch commits)
  1951→
  1952→*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  1953→----
  1954→jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX: commit message
  1955→
  1956→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1957→----
  1958→
  1959→No action code for standard commits. The {jjdt_coronet} provides full context.
  1960→
  1961→*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  1962→----
  1963→jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:A: description
  1964→
  1965→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1966→----
  1967→
  1968→Pace-level action codes:
  1969→
  1970→* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  1971→* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
  1972→* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (primed pace)
  1973→* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  1974→
  1975→*Heat-level operations*:
  1976→----
  1977→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:N: heat-silks
  1978→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:S: pace-silks
  1979→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:r: reordered
  1980→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:T: pace-silks
  1981→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
  1982→jjb:BRAND:₣XX:R: heat-silks
  1983→----
  1984→
  1985→Heat-level action codes:
  1986→
  1987→* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
  1988→* `S` — Slate: add new pace
  1989→* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  1990→* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
  1991→* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  1992→* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  1993→
  1994→[[jjdo_rein]]
  1995→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  1996→==== {jjdo_rein}
  1997→
  1998→Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
  1999→{jjdhr_heat}.
  2000→
  2001→{jjds_arguments}
  2002→
  2003→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2004→* {jjdt_firemark}
  2005→(positional) — target
  2006→{jjdhr_heat}
  2007→
  2008→// ⟦axd_optional⟧
  2009→* `--limit` — maximum entries to return (default: 50)
  2010→
  2011→{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  2012→[source,json]
  2013→----
  2014→[
  2015→  {
  2016→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2017→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2018→    "subject": "commit message"
  2019→  },
  2020→  {
  2021→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2022→    "coronet": "₢XXXXX",
  2023→    "action": "A",
  2024→    "subject": "description"
  2025→  },
  2026→  {
  2027→    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2028→    "action": "N",
  2029→    "subject": "heat-silks"
  2030→  }
  2031→]
  2032→----
  2033→Field `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
  2034→Field `action` is present for chalk/heat operations, absent for standard notch commits.
  2035→Entries ordered newest first.
  2036→
  2037→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2038→
  2039→{jjds_behavior}
  2040→
  2041→. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:BRAND:(₣XX|₢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  2042→. Parse each line: extract timestamp, identity, action (if present), and message
  2043→. For pace-level (₢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
  2044→. For heat-level (₣FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
  2045→. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  2046→. Output JSON
  2047→
  2048→[[jjdo_notch]]
  2049→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2050→==== {jjdo_notch}
  2051→
  2052→Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  2053→
  2054→{jjds_arguments}
  2055→
  2056→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2057→* {jjdt_coronet}
  2058→(positional) — active pace identity (embeds parent heat)
  2059→
  2060→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2061→
  2062→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2063→
  2064→{jjds_behavior}
  2065→
  2066→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2067→. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
  2068→. Run size guard check
  2069→. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
  2070→. Format full message: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2071→. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  2072→. Release lock
  2073→. Output commit hash
  2074→
  2075→[[jjdo_chalk]]
  2076→// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
  2077→==== {jjdo_chalk}
  2078→
  2079→Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.
  2080→
  2081→{jjds_arguments}
  2082→
  2083→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2084→* identity (positional) — {jjdt_coronet} for pace-level, {jjdt_firemark} for heat-level discussion
  2085→
  2086→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2087→* `--marker` — marker type: `A`, `W`, `F`, or `d` (single letter codes)
  2088→
  2089→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  2090→* `--description` — marker description text
  2091→
  2092→{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2093→
  2094→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2095→
  2096→{jjds_behavior}
  2097→
  2098→. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2099→. Detect identity type by length (5 chars = coronet, 2 chars = firemark)
  2100→. Validate: `A`/`W`/`F` require coronet; `d` accepts either
  2101→. Format message:
  2102→.. If coronet: `jjb:BRAND:₢XXXXX:X: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2103→.. If firemark (discussion only): `jjb:BRAND:₣XX:d: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2104→. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
  2105→. Release lock
  2106→. Output commit hash
  2107→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

= Job Jockey Gallops Data Model
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// jjdt_:  Types (scalar value types with validation rules)
// jjdgr_: Gallops record
// jjdgm_: Gallops members
// jjdhr_: Heat record
// jjdhm_: Heat members
// jjdhe_: Heat enum values (status)
// jjdpr_: Pace record
// jjdpm_: Pace members
// jjdkr_: Tack record
// jjdkm_: Tack members
// jjdpe_: Tack state enum values
// jjdo_:  Operations
// jjds_:  Section headers (operation documentation structure)
// jjdz_:  Serialization concerns
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

// Types
:jjdt_coronet:    <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet>>
:jjdt_coronet_p:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet's>>
:jjdt_coronet_s:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronets>>
:jjdt_firemark:   <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark>>
:jjdt_firemark_p: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark's>>
:jjdt_firemark_s: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemarks>>
:jjdt_silks:      <<jjdt_silks,Silks>>

// Gallops record and members
:jjdgm_seed:        <<jjdgm_seed,next_heat_seed>>
:jjdgr_gallops:     <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops>>
:jjdgr_gallops_p:   <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops'>>

// Heat record and members
:jjdhe_current:   <<jjdhe_current,current>>
:jjdhe_retired:   <<jjdhe_retired,retired>>
:jjdhm_created:   <<jjdhm_created,creation_time>>
:jjdhm_firemark:  <<jjdhm_firemark,firemark>>
:jjdhm_order:     <<jjdhm_order,order>>
:jjdhm_paddock:   <<jjdhm_paddock,paddock_file>>
:jjdhm_seed:      <<jjdhm_seed,next_pace_seed>>
:jjdhm_silks:     <<jjdhm_silks,silks>>
:jjdhm_status:    <<jjdhm_status,status>>
:jjdhr_heat:      <<jjdhr_heat,Heat>>
:jjdhr_heat_p:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heat's>>
:jjdhr_heat_s:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heats>>

// Pace record and members
:jjdpm_coronet:   <<jjdpm_coronet,coronet>>
:jjdpm_silks:     <<jjdpm_silks,silks>>
:jjdpr_pace:      <<jjdpr_pace,Pace>>
:jjdpr_pace_p:    <<jjdpr_pace,Pace's>>
:jjdpr_pace_s:    <<jjdpr_pace,Paces>>

// Pace/Tack state enum values (used in Tack)
:jjdpe_abandoned: <<jjdpe_abandoned,abandoned>>
:jjdpe_complete:  <<jjdpe_complete,complete>>
:jjdpe_primed:    <<jjdpe_primed,primed>>
:jjdpe_rough:     <<jjdpe_rough,rough>>

// Tack record and members
:jjdkm_direction: <<jjdkm_direction,direction>>
:jjdkm_refined:   <<jjdkm_refined,ts>>
:jjdkm_state:     <<jjdkm_state,state>>
:jjdkm_text:      <<jjdkm_text,text>>
:jjdkr_tack:      <<jjdkr_tack,Tack>>
:jjdkr_tack_p:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tack's>>
:jjdkr_tack_s:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tacks>>

// Arguments
:jjda_after:          <<jjda_after,--after>>
:jjda_before:         <<jjda_before,--before>>
:jjda_created:        <<jjda_created,--created>>
:jjda_direction:      <<jjda_direction,--direction>>
:jjda_file:           <<jjda_file,--file>>
:jjda_first:          <<jjda_first,--first>>
:jjda_last:           <<jjda_last,--last>>
:jjda_move:           <<jjda_move,--move>>
:jjda_pace:           <<jjda_pace,--pace>>
:jjda_silks:          <<jjda_silks,--silks>>
:jjda_state:          <<jjda_state,--state>>
:jjda_status:         <<jjda_status,--status>>
:jjda_to:             <<jjda_to,--to>>

// CLI executor
:jjdx_vvx:   <<jjdx_vvx,vvx>>
:jjdx_cli:   <<jjdx_cli,jjx>>

// Operations
:jjdo_canter:         <<jjdo_canter,jjx_canter>>
:jjdo_chalk:          <<jjdo_chalk,jjx_chalk>>
:jjdo_draft:          <<jjdo_draft,jjx_draft>>
:jjdo_muster:         <<jjdo_muster,jjx_muster>>
:jjdo_nominate:       <<jjdo_nominate,jjx_nominate>>
:jjdo_notch:          <<jjdo_notch,jjx_notch>>
:jjdo_rail:           <<jjdo_rail,jjx_rail>>
:jjdo_rein:           <<jjdo_rein,jjx_rein>>
:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>
:jjdo_slate:          <<jjdo_slate,jjx_slate>>
:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>
:jjdo_validate:       <<jjdo_validate,jjx_validate>>

// Section headers
:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>
:jjds_behavior:         <<jjds_behavior,Behavior:>>
:jjds_exit_enumerated:  <<jjds_exit_enumerated,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_exit_uniform:     <<jjds_exit_uniform,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_stdout:           <<jjds_stdout,Stdout:>>

// Serialization
:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).
Each
{jjdpr_pace}
maintains a history of
{jjdkr_tack_s}
(plan refinements).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
The internet is full of bad bash.
Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

The
{jjdkr_tack}
history within each
{jjdpr_pace}
exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
Git has the full history, but
{jjdkr_tack_s}
let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
prepend to position 0 rather than append.
This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.

The pattern:
[source,bash]
----
lock_acquire
vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
lock_release
----

This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.

=== Deterministic Serialization

Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
This ensures that reordering paces via
{jjdo_rail}
produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.

=== Identity Encoding

{jjdt_firemark}
(`₣` prefix) and
{jjdt_coronet}
(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.

=== Horse Racing Vocabulary

Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.

* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)

== Types

[[jjdt_firemark]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_firemark}::
The identity of a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_coronet]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_coronet}::
The identity of a
{jjdpr_pace}.
Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
{jjdhr_heat_p}
encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
+
*Reassignment:* When a
{jjdpr_pace}
is moved between
{jjdhr_heat_s}
via
{jjdo_draft},
the old
{jjdt_coronet}
becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
{jjdhr_heat_p}
{jjdhm_seed}.
The new
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds the destination
{jjdt_firemark}.
{jjdkr_tack}
history is preserved, with a note recording the original
{jjdt_coronet}.

[[jjdt_silks]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{jjdt_silks}::
A kebab-case display name.
Used for human-readable identification of
{jjdhr_heat_s}
and
{jjdpr_pace_s}.
Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`

== Records

=== {jjdgr_gallops}

[[jjdgr_gallops]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdgr_gallops}::
The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

==== Members

* [[jjdgm_seed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdgm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_firemark}
to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_nominate}.

=== {jjdhr_heat}

[[jjdhr_heat]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdhr_heat}::
A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
Contains zero or more
{jjdpr_pace_s}
representing discrete actions.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_firemark}.

==== Members

* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_firemark}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
primary key.
Type:
{jjdt_firemark}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdhm_silks]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_silks}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

* [[jjdhm_created]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_created}
records when the
{jjdhr_heat}
was nominated.
Format: YYMMDD.

* [[jjdhm_status]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_status}
indicates the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
lifecycle state.
Values:
{jjdhe_current}
or
{jjdhe_retired}.

* [[jjdhm_order]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
{jjdhm_order}
lists
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in execution sequence.
May be reordered via
{jjdo_rail}.

* [[jjdhm_seed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_coronet}
index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_slate}.

* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdhm_paddock}
is the path to the paddock file containing
{jjdhr_heat}
context and background.
Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
{jjdt_firemark}
(without `₣` prefix).
Created by
{jjdo_nominate}.

==== Status Values

[[jjdhe_current]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdhe_current}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is active and accepting work.

[[jjdhe_retired]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdhe_retired}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is complete and archived.

=== {jjdpr_pace}

[[jjdpr_pace]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdpr_pace}::
A discrete action within a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Contains one or more
{jjdkr_tack_s}
representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_coronet}.

==== Members

* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdpm_coronet}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
globally unique identity.
Type:
{jjdt_coronet}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdpm_silks]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdpm_silks}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

=== {jjdkr_tack}

[[jjdkr_tack]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
{jjdkr_tack}::
A snapshot of
{jjdpr_pace}
state and plan at a point in time.
{jjdkr_tack_s}
form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
Position 0 is the most recent; older
{jjdkr_tack_s}
follow.
New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
The current
{jjdpr_pace}
state is always
`tacks[0].state`.

==== Members

* [[jjdkm_refined]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdkm_refined}
records when this
{jjdkr_tack}
was added.
Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.

* [[jjdkm_state]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdkm_state}
indicates the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
progress at this snapshot.
Values:
{jjdpe_rough},
{jjdpe_primed},
{jjdpe_complete},
or
{jjdpe_abandoned}.

* [[jjdkm_text]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
{jjdkm_text}
contains the plan specification.
Non-empty string describing what the
{jjdpr_pace}
should accomplish.

* [[jjdkm_direction]]
// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional⟧
{jjdkm_direction}
contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
Required when
{jjdkm_state}
is
{jjdpe_primed};
forbidden otherwise.
Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.

==== State Values

[[jjdpe_rough]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_rough}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
needs clarification before work can proceed.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_rough}
pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.

[[jjdpe_primed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_primed}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
is ready for autonomous execution.
The
{jjdkm_direction}
field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_primed}
pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.

[[jjdpe_complete]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_complete}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
has been successfully finished.

[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
{jjdpe_abandoned}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
was stopped without completion.

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

== Arguments

*Note:* {jjda_file} appears as the first argument on all operations.
It is annotated `// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧` and uses the default value when not explicitly provided.

[[jjda_file]]

{jjdgr_gallops}

== CLI

[[jjdx_vvx]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_program⟧
{jjdx_vvx}::
The Voce Viva executor.
Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.

[[jjdx_cli]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_command_group⟧
{jjdx_cli}::
The Job Jockey command group within
{jjdx_vvx}.
Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
All operations are subcommands of
{jjdx_cli}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.

== Operations

Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access (read, transform, atomic write).
Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
{jjda_file}.

[[jjdo_rail]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_rail}

Reorder
{jjdpr_pace_s}
within a
{jjdhr_heat}
by updating
{jjdhm_order}.
Supports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
{jjdpr_pace}.

*Order mode* (full replacement):
----
vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> <CORONET>...
----

*Move mode* (single relocation):
----
vvx jjx_rail <FIREMARK> --move <CORONET> [--before <TARGET> | --after <TARGET> | --first | --last]
----

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) — target
{jjdhr_heat}

*Order mode arguments:*

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* order — array of
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in desired sequence (space-separated positional arguments)

*Move mode arguments:*

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_move}
— {jjdt_coronet}
to relocate

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* Exactly one positioning flag:
{jjda_before},
{jjda_after},
{jjda_first},
or
{jjda_last}

*Mode detection:* If
{jjda_move}
is present, move mode; otherwise order mode.

{jjds_stdout} Updated
{jjdhm_order}
array, one
{jjdt_coronet}
per line, in new sequence.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

*Common steps:*

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Verify
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}

*Order mode:*

. Parse positional arguments into array of
{jjdt_coronet_s}
. Validate order array:
.. Count must match current
{jjdhm_order}
length
.. No duplicate
{jjdt_coronet_s}
.. All
{jjdt_coronet_s}
must exist in
{jjdhr_heat_p}
`paces` object
.. All
{jjdt_coronet_s}
must embed correct parent
{jjdt_firemark}
. Replace
{jjdhm_order}
with new order array
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdhm_order},
one
{jjdt_coronet}
per line

*Move mode:*

. Validate
{jjda_move}
{jjdt_coronet}
exists in
{jjdhr_heat}
. Validate exactly one positioning flag provided
. If
{jjda_before}
or
{jjda_after}:
validate target
{jjdt_coronet}
exists and differs from
{jjda_move}
{jjdt_coronet}
. Remove
{jjda_move}
{jjdt_coronet}
from current position in
{jjdhm_order}
. Insert at new position:
.. If
{jjda_first}:
prepend to beginning
.. If
{jjda_last}:
append to end
.. If
{jjda_before}:
insert immediately before target
{jjdt_coronet}
.. If
{jjda_after}:
insert immediately after target
{jjdt_coronet}
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdhm_order},
one
{jjdt_coronet}
per line

*Validation errors:*

[cols="2,3"]
|===
| Condition | Error

| {jjda_move} with positional coronets
| "Cannot combine --move with positional coronets"

| {jjda_move} {jjdt_coronet} not found
| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"

| No positioning flag provided
| "Move mode requires exactly one positioning flag"

| Multiple positioning flags
| "Conflicting positioning flags: {flags}"

| Target same as move
| "Cannot position pace relative to itself"

| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found
| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
|===

[[jjdo_draft]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧
==== {jjdo_draft}

Move a
{jjdpr_pace}
from one
{jjdhr_heat}
to another.
This is a primitive operation: mechanical transfer with no ceremony.
The
{jjdpr_pace}
receives a new
{jjdt_coronet}
in the destination
{jjdhr_heat};
all
{jjdkr_tack}
history is preserved.

*Important:* "Drafted" is not a
{jjdpr_pace}
state.
{jjdpr_pace}
states remain:
{jjdpe_rough},
{jjdpe_primed},
{jjdpe_complete},
{jjdpe_abandoned}.
Draft is an operation that moves
{jjdpr_pace_s}
between
{jjdhr_heat_s};
state is preserved through the move.

{jjds_arguments}

// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
* {jjda_file}

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjdt_coronet}
(positional) —
{jjdpr_pace}
to move

// ⟦axd_required⟧
* {jjda_to}
— destination
{jjdt_firemark}

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_before}
— insert before specified
{jjdt_coronet}
in destination

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_after}
— insert after specified
{jjdt_coronet}
in destination

// ⟦axd_optional⟧
* {jjda_first}
— insert at beginning of destination
{jjdhm_order}

*Positioning:* Only one of
{jjda_before},
{jjda_after},
or
{jjda_first}
may be specified.
If none provided, append to end of destination
{jjdhm_order}
(default).

{jjds_stdout} New
{jjdt_coronet}
in destination
{jjdhr_heat}

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file
. Extract source
{jjdt_firemark}
from
{jjdt_coronet}
(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
. Verify source
{jjdhr_heat}
exists
. Verify
{jjdpr_pace}
exists in source
{jjdhr_heat}
. Verify destination
{jjdhr_heat}
exists with given
{jjdt_firemark}
. If
{jjda_before}
or
{jjda_after}
provided, verify target
{jjdt_coronet}
exists in destination
{jjdhr_heat}
. Read destination
{jjdhm_seed}
. Construct new
{jjdt_coronet}:
`₢` + destination
{jjdt_firemark}
(2 chars) + destination
{jjdhm_seed}
(3 chars)
. Copy
{jjdpr_pace}
data to destination with new
{jjdt_coronet}:
.. Preserve
{jjdpm_silks}
.. Prepend new
{jjdkr_tack}
recording the draft:
... {jjdkm_refined}:
current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
... {jjdkm_state}:
inherited from previous `tacks[0].state`
... {jjdkm_text}:
`Drafted from {old_coronet} in {source_firemark}.\n\n` + previous `tacks[0].text`
.. Preserve remaining
{jjdkr_tack}
history
. Insert new
{jjdt_coronet}
into destination
{jjdhm_order}
at determined position
. Increment destination
{jjdhm_seed}
. Remove
{jjdpr_pace}
from source
{jjdhr_heat}:
.. Remove from source `paces` object
.. Remove from source
{jjdhm_order}
. Write transformed
{jjdgr_gallops}
to file (atomic)
. Output new
{jjdt_coronet}
to stdout

*Validation errors:*

[cols="2,3"]
|===
| Condition | Error

| Source and destination same
| "Cannot draft pace to same heat"

| Source {jjdpr_pace} not found
| "Pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"

| Destination {jjdhr_heat} not found
| "Heat {firemark} not found"

| Target {jjdt_coronet} not found in destination
| "Target pace {coronet} not found in heat {firemark}"
|===

[[jjdo_tally]]
// ⟦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand⟧

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file

{jjdhr_heat}
exists

{jjdpr_pace}

from `tacks[0]`
